channel.title: NPR Topics: Culture
channel.description: Art and entertainment commentary plus interviews, book reviews, movie reviews, music reviews, comedy, and visual art. Subscribe to podcasts and follow trends in music, painting, art, architecture, photography, and design.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Jigsaw puzzling is a growing competitive activity — with a national tournament
item.description: There's a big national competition in Atlanta this week in puzzling. Teams will race to put jigsaw pieces into place, assembling 500 and 1,000 piece puzzles they've never seen.
item.content:encoded: There's a big national competition in Atlanta this week in puzzling. Teams will race to put jigsaw pieces into place, assembling 500 and 1,000 piece puzzles they've never seen.
item.dc:creator: Leslie E. Thompson

item.title: 'The Madison' adds to Taylor Sheridan's 'Yellowstone' legacy — 'Marshals' not so much
item.description: Yellowstone's creator is back with two new shows set in the American West. Marshals struggles, but The Madison offers a thoughtful portrait of a family in flux.
item.content:encoded:
Yellowstone's creator is back with two new shows set in the American West. Marshals struggles, but The Madison offers a thoughtful portrait of a family in flux.

(Image credit: Emerson Miller)
item.dc:creator: David Bianculli

item.title: Strangers forge a powerful bond after a car crash in 'Miroirs No. 3'
item.description: After the sudden death of her boyfriend, a young Berlin woman is taken in by a family she meets in the countryside. In showing the ache of love and loss, Miroirs No. 3 holds up a mirror to us all.
item.content:encoded: After the sudden death of her boyfriend, a young Berlin woman is taken in by a family she meets in the countryside. In showing the ache of love and loss, Miroirs No. 3 holds up a mirror to us all.
item.dc:creator: Justin Chang

item.title: Remembering action hero Chuck Norris
item.description: In addition to his kung fu and action films, Norris, who died March 19, starred in the TV show Walker, Texas Ranger. He spoke with Terry Gross in 1988 about learning karate while stationed in Korea.
item.content:encoded: In addition to his kung fu and action films, Norris, who died March 19, starred in the TV show Walker, Texas Ranger. He spoke with Terry Gross in 1988 about learning karate while stationed in Korea.
item.dc:creator: Terry Gross

item.title: Comedian Julio Torres shares a moment of pride he experienced as a child
item.description: On Wild Card, well-known guests answer the kinds of questions we often think about but don't talk about. Comedian Julio Torres talks about a moment of pride he experienced as a child.
item.content:encoded: On Wild Card, well-known guests answer the kinds of questions we often think about but don't talk about. Comedian Julio Torres talks about a moment of pride he experienced as a child.

item.title: 'Sueño Perro' pulls from the unused scraps of Iñárritu's groundbreaking film
item.description: In 2000, filmmaker Alejandro Iñárritu made waves at Cannes with Amores Perros. He's now turned the film's extra footage into a remarkable art installation at the Los Angeles County Museum of Art.
item.content:encoded: In 2000, filmmaker Alejandro Iñárritu made waves at Cannes with Amores Perros. He's now turned the film's extra footage into a remarkable art installation at the Los Angeles County Museum of Art.
item.dc:creator: Carolina Miranda

item.title: Suit asks court to force Trump administration to use 'The Kennedy Center' name
item.description: The motion is part of a lawsuit challenging President Trump and the Center's board, who now refer to the complex as "The Trump Kennedy Center."
item.content:encoded:
The motion is part of a lawsuit challenging President Trump and the Center's board, who now refer to the complex as "The Trump Kennedy Center."

(Image credit: Jim Watson)
item.dc:creator: Anastasia Tsioulcas

item.title: Bill Maher is getting the Mark Twain Prize after all
item.description: There was confusion about whether the satirist would be getting the Kennedy Center's top humor award after White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt called it "fake news." Now it's confirmed.
item.content:encoded:
There was confusion about whether the satirist would be getting the Kennedy Center's top humor award after White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt called it "fake news." Now it's confirmed.

(Image credit: Evan Agostini)
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Blair

item.title: Decades ago, a Maryland sailor burned his winter socks. Now it's a spring tradition
item.description: In Annapolis, Md., people gather each year to usher in the warmer weather by burning their socks. The springtime tradition is the unofficial start of the Chesapeake Bay sailing season.
item.content:encoded:
In Annapolis, Md., people gather each year to usher in the warmer weather by burning their socks. The springtime tradition is the unofficial start of the Chesapeake Bay sailing season.

(Image credit: Tyrone Turner)
item.dc:creator: Scott Neuman

item.title: Stephen Colbert's next epic quest? Writing a new 'Lord of the Rings' movie
item.description: The film will focus on chapters in J.R.R. Tolkien's The Fellowship of the Ring that were left out of the first movie in the trilogy.
item.content:encoded:
The film will focus on chapters in J.R.R. Tolkien's The Fellowship of the Ring that were left out of the first movie in the trilogy.

(Image credit: Angela Weiss)
item.dc:creator: Ivy Buck
